examples.title: Queue Array Implementation - Basic Operations
examples.description: Pseudo-code for array-based queue with enqueue, dequeue, and status checking operations
examples.code:
CONSTANT MAXSIZE = 100
ARRAY queue[MAXSIZE]
INTEGER front = -1
INTEGER rear = -1

FUNCTION isEmpty()
    IF front == -1 OR front > rear THEN
        RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
        RETURN FALSE
    END IF
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION isFull()
    IF rear == MAXSIZE - 1 THEN
        RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
        RETURN FALSE
    END IF
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION enqueue(element)
    IF isFull() THEN
        PRINT "Queue Overflow"
        RETURN
    END IF
    IF front == -1 THEN
        front = 0
    END IF
    rear = rear + 1
    queue[rear] = element
    PRINT "Enqueued: " + element
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION dequeue()
    IF isEmpty() THEN
        PRINT "Queue Underflow"
        RETURN NULL
    END IF
    element = queue[front]
    front = front + 1
    IF front > rear THEN
        front = -1
        rear = -1
    END IF
    RETURN element
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This pseudo-code demonstrates basic queue operations using array. The isEmpty and isFull functions check queue status. Enqueue adds element at rear and dequeue removes from front. When front exceeds rear after dequeue, queue is reset to empty state.

examples.title: Circular Queue Operations
examples.description: Pseudo-code for circular queue implementation overcoming linear queue limitations
examples.code:
CONSTANT MAXSIZE = 100
ARRAY queue[MAXSIZE]
INTEGER front = -1
INTEGER rear = -1

FUNCTION isEmpty()
    IF front == -1 THEN
        RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
        RETURN FALSE
    END IF
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION isFull()
    IF (rear + 1) % MAXSIZE == front THEN
        RETURN TRUE
    ELSE
        RETURN FALSE
    END IF
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION enqueue(element)
    IF isFull() THEN
        PRINT "Queue Overflow"
        RETURN
    END IF
    IF front == -1 THEN
        front = 0
        rear = 0
    ELSE
        rear = (rear + 1) % MAXSIZE
    END IF
    queue[rear] = element
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION dequeue()
    IF isEmpty() THEN
        PRINT "Queue Underflow"
        RETURN NULL
    END IF
    element = queue[front]
    IF front == rear THEN
        front = -1
        rear = -1
    ELSE
        front = (front + 1) % MAXSIZE
    END IF
    RETURN element
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Circular queue uses modular arithmetic to wrap rear and front pointers around. The condition (rear+1)%MAXSIZE == front indicates full queue. This eliminates the phantom overflow problem of linear queues by reusing deleted spaces.

examples.title: Queue Implementation in C
examples.description: Complete C program implementing queue using array with full operation support
examples.explanation: This C program implements queue using static array. The isEmpty and isFull functions check queue status. Enqueue increments rear and stores element. Dequeue returns element at front and increments front. When queue becomes empty after dequeue, both pointers are reset to -1.

examples.title: Queue Implementation in Python
examples.description: Python implementation of queue using list with class-based approach
examples.code:
class Queue:
    def __init__(self, maxsize=100):
        self.queue = []
        self.maxsize = maxsize
        self.front = -1
        self.rear = -1
    
    def is_empty(self):
        return self.front == -1 or self.front > self.rear
    
    def is_full(self):
        return self.rear == self.maxsize - 1
    
    def enqueue(self, element):
        if self.is_full():
            print("Queue Overflow!")
            return
        if self.front == -1:
            self.front = 0
        self.rear += 1
        self.queue.append(element)
        print(f"Enqueued: {element}")
    
    def dequeue(self):
        if self.is_empty():
            print("Queue Underflow!")
            return None
        element = self.queue[self.front]
        self.front += 1
        if self.front > self.rear:
            self.front = self.rear = -1
            self.queue = []
        return element
    
    def display(self):
        if self.is_empty():
            print("Queue is empty")
            return
        print("Queue:", self.queue[self.front:self.rear+1])

# Usage
q = Queue()
q.enqueue(10)
q.enqueue(20)
q.enqueue(30)
q.display()
print(f"Dequeued: {q.dequeue()}")
q.display()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation uses a class to encapsulate queue operations. List is used for storage. The enqueue method adds elements at the end and dequeue removes from the front. Python's list append operation provides amortized O(1) complexity.
